MINI MINUTES: Appearances via CourtCall:
Mark Kenney, Esq. and Laszlo Ladi, Esq., 415-398-3344, appearing for Marina Galli, Plaintiff
Mark Chuang, Esq., 925-837-2317, appearing for Pacific Union, for Defendant.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL, INC. Petition For Order To Vacate Portions Of The Arbitration Award is OFF CALENDAR. Duplicate entry.Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Petition For An Order To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via CourtCall:
Mark Kenney, Esq. and Laszlo Ladi, Esq., 415-398-3344, appearing for Marina Galli, Plaintiff
Mark Chuang, Esq., 925-837-2317, appearing for Pacific Union, for Defendant.
Eric Saw, Esq., 415-536-2800, appearing for Peggy Burnham

ARGUED. Mr. Chuang stated that he contested to the tentative ruling and presented his oral argument in support of his position. Mr. Kenny provided case background. Mr. Shaw requested dismissal from this petition. Mr. Kenny responded that it does not work that way.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER MARINA GALLI Petition For An Order To Confirm Arbitration Award is GRANTED.
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
CPF-18-516400 Marina Galli VS. Pacific Union International Inc. and
CPF-20-517091 Peggy Conroy Burnham VS. Marina Galli.

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Laszlo Ladi ll@severson.com for Petitioner/Respondent Marina Galli.
Alexander Weyand for Petitioner/Respondent Peggy Conroy Burnham.
Mark Chuang for Respondent Pacific Union International Inc.

Discussed (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER MARINA GALLI's Petition For An Order To Confirm Arbitration Award

Transferred to be heard in department 501 at 9:30 a.m. on September 29, 2020, with the petition to vacate arbitration award that is currently set in department 501. That department handles all matters arising from the acquisition of real property per SF Local Rule 8.10(A)(1).

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
